channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sadie Sink Joins 'Spider-Man: Brand New Day' in Surprise Role
item.description: Sadie Sink, known for her role in 'Stranger Things,' has been cast in a surprise role in the upcoming Spider-Man film 'Brand New Day' without undergoing a traditional audition process. The news, revea...

item.title: Dave Chappelle discusses America, defends Haitian migrants, and criticizes Trump’s rhetoric
item.description: Comedian Dave Chappelle made headlines after addressing political and social issues during an appearance on CNN’s “Independence Eve” coverage, hosted by Anderson Cooper and Andy Cohen. During the segm...

item.title: AI's Impact on Literature and the Role of Human Creativity
item.description: The article explores the growing influence of artificial intelligence on the literary world, highlighting concerns about homogenization and the erosion of human creativity. It discusses how AI tools, ...

item.title: NASA's PROMISE rover: A Mars prototype repurposed for lunar exploration
item.description: The article discusses NASA's consideration of repurposing a Mars rover replica, named PROMISE, for lunar missions. Originally designed as a Mars exploration vehicle, PROMISE is a modified version of t...

item.title: AI-Generated Code and Copyright Challenges in Open Source Projects
item.description: The article discusses the legal and ethical challenges posed by AI-generated code in open-source software development. It highlights how AI tools produce code with uncertain copyright protections, thr...

item.title: UNDP, Lagos push for global market access for fashion and leather SMEs
item.description: The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) and Lagos State Government have called for stronger investment, better financing, and wider market access to help grow Africa’s fashion, leather, and cr...

item.title: UK Heatwave Expected to Reach 31°C This Week
item.description: The UK is bracing for another heatwave as temperatures are forecast to rise again, with the capital facing scorching conditions. The Met Office has warned that while the south and southeast will remai...

item.title: Shuvee Etrata's Sister Shaina Becomes First Family Graduate
item.description: Shuvee Etrata, a prominent Filipino celebrity, recently celebrated her sister Shaina's graduation as the first member of their family to earn a degree. This milestone marks a significant achievement f...

item.title: Ebonyi NDC commits to fielding own candidate for 2027 governorship
item.description: The Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC) in Ebonyi State has officially distanced itself from any endorsement of a Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) governorship aspirant ahead of the 2027 elections. The pa...

item.title: A closer look at the personal flaws and controversies of the U.S. Founding Fathers on the 250th anniversary of independence
item.description: The article reflects on the 250th anniversary of the United States and challenges the idealized image of the Founding Fathers by highlighting their personal flaws, scandals, and human contradictions. ...

item.title: India’s previous T20I matches at Old Trafford: results from 2011 and 2018 ahead of ENG vs IND 2026
item.description: India have played only a limited number of T20 International matches at Old Trafford in Manchester, making their upcoming 2026 encounter against England a notable return to the venue after several yea...

item.title: South Africa Commends Proteas Women for Excellence in ICC Women's T20 World Cup Despite Loss
item.description: The South African government has expressed admiration for the Proteas Women cricket team's performance in the ICC Women's T20 World Cup, despite their defeat to England by 40 runs in the final. The Go...

item.title: Ukrainian Woman Under Investigation for Monaco Bomb Attack
item.description: The article discusses an error message encountered when attempting to access a news story about a police investigation into a Ukrainian woman suspected of involvement in a bomb attack in Monaco. News ...

item.title: Wallabies Begin Test Season with Ireland Match in Sydney
item.description: The Wallabies face Ireland in their Test season opener at Allianz Stadium, marking a significant match for coach Joe Schmidt's final Test. Despite past inconsistencies, the team aims to build on their...

item.title: Aurangzeb's Complex Legacy as a Mughal Emperor
item.description: The article provides a nuanced analysis of Aurangzeb, the 17th-century Mughal emperor, challenging simplistic narratives of him as either a destroyer of Hindu civilization or a defender of Muslim iden...

item.title: Zari Hassan Addresses Claims of Being Jonasi's Female Version
item.description: South Africa-based Ugandan socialite Zari Hassan has publicly dismissed claims by Kenyans on social media who compare her to Jonasi Gomora, the toxic protagonist of Netflix's The Polygamist. In a TikT...

item.title: The Future of Democracy and AI Governance
item.description: The original text explores complex themes of political philosophy, democracy, and the role of artificial intelligence in governance. It critiques traditional democratic systems, referencing Plato's 'p...

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce Tie the Knot at Madison Square Garden Ceremony
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce married in a high-profile ceremony at Madison Square Garden, with actor Adam Sandler serving as the officiant. The event, shrouded in secrecy, featured a star-studded gue...

item.title: House prosecution says VP Sara Duterte’s attendance will not impact impeachment case
item.description: The House prosecution team has stated that Vice President Sara Duterte’s decision to appear or not appear at her impeachment trial will not affect the strength or progress of their case. According to ...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Sharif Encourages Turkish Investment in Key Sectors
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has invited Turkish companies to expand their investments in Pakistan across sectors like energy, infrastructure, and IT. During his official visit to Turkey, he held mee...
